methods.description: Procedure for preparing pollen tubes and acquiring Fourier-transform infrared (FTIR) spectra from specific regions (apical and sub-apical) to analyze cell wall modifications, focusing on the 800-1800 cm⁻¹ range. The method involves sample preparation, spectral acquisition using a Nicolet continuum FTIR microscope, and subsequent analysis of the relevant spectral range.

steps.description: After being cultured for 2 h, the pollen tubes were collected
steps.searchable_summary: Culture pollen tubes for a specified duration and collect them

steps.description: and washed three times with deionized water
steps.searchable_summary: Wash collected pollen tubes multiple times with deionized water

steps.description: and then dried on a silvergilt slide at room temperature.
steps.searchable_summary: Dry washed pollen tubes on a silvergilt slide at room temperature
outputs: dried pollen tubes on silvergilt slide

steps.description: Infrared spectra were obtained from 20x320 lm areas in the apical region (clear zone)... under a Nicolet continuum FTIR microscope (Thermo Electron Corporation, USA) equipped with a mercury-cadmium-telluride detector.
steps.searchable_summary: Acquire FTIR spectra from the apical region of dried pollen tubes using a specified FTIR microscope
inputs: dried pollen tubes on silvergilt slide

steps.description: ...and sub-apical (;130-150 lm from the tube tip) region (organelle and nuclear zone) of the pollen tubes, respectively, under a Nicolet continuum FTIR microscope...
steps.searchable_summary: Acquire FTIR spectra from the sub-apical region of dried pollen tubes using a specified FTIR microscope
inputs: dried pollen tubes on silvergilt slide

steps.description: The spectra were recorded at a resolution of 8 cm À1 with 64 coadded interferegrams.
steps.searchable_summary: Record FTIR spectra with specified resolution and number of coadded interferograms

steps.description: The spectrum between 800 and 1800 cm À1 , which contains information of polysaccharides, was selected in order to monitor cell wall modifications.
steps.searchable_summary: Select or filter the FTIR spectrum to include only the range between 800 and 1800 cm⁻¹

steps.description: Assignments of the main bands in FTIR spectra were taken from these sources (Synytsya et al., 2003;Toole et al., 2004;Barron et al., 2005;Jones et al., 2005;Zhang et al., 2005;Sheng et al., 2006).
steps.searchable_summary: Assign main spectral bands in the filtered FTIR spectra based on cited literature

steps.description: Experiments were repeated at least three times with three or four replicates in each group each time.
steps.searchable_summary: Repeat the entire experimental procedure multiple times with biological replicates
